title: What is Neural Style Transfer?
concept: Neural Style Transfer combines the content of one image with the artistic style of another, using a CNN to separate 'what' an image shows from 'how' it's painted.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What does SPICE evaluate when scoring an image caption?

options.text: The semantic scene graph — objects, attributes, and relationships
The exact pixel values of the image
The file size of the caption text

exercises.explain: Yes! SPICE checks semantic structure, not exact wording.

cards.title: Turning a photo into a Van Gogh painting
cards.body: Imagine taking a photo of your street and making it look like it was painted by Van Gogh — same houses and cars, but swirling brushstrokes and bold colors. Neural Style Transfer does exactly this using a CNN.

cards.title: Neural Style Transfer
cards.body: Neural Style Transfer generates a new image that keeps the content (layout, objects) of one image while adopting the style (colors, textures, brushstrokes) of another, using a pretrained CNN's internal features.

cards.title: Content vs. the frame's paint job
cards.body: Think of a house's blueprint (content — where the walls and rooms go) versus its paint color and wallpaper (style — the visual texture). Style transfer keeps the blueprint from photo A and repaints it using photo B's palette.

cards.title: Starry Night filter
cards.body: Feed the model a photo of a bridge (content image) and Van Gogh's 'Starry Night' (style image). The output is your bridge, but rendered with Starry Night's swirling yellow-blue brushstrokes.

cards.title: It's not a simple filter overlay
cards.body: It's tempting to think this is just an Instagram filter blending two images. In reality, the network optimizes a brand new image pixel-by-pixel to match content and style statistics — it's a search process, not a blend.

left.text:
Layout, objects, shapes
Comes from deep CNN layers
The 'what' of the image

right.text:
Colors, textures, brushstrokes
Comes from shallow CNN layers
The 'how' of the image

exercises.prompt: What does Neural Style Transfer combine?

options.text: The content of one image with the style of another

exercises.explain: Correct! It merges content and style from two separate images.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Style transfer is a simple pixel overlay filter.
exercises.explain: False. It's an optimization process that generates a brand-new image matching content and style targets.

exercises.sentence: Style transfer keeps the ___ of one image while adopting the style of another.
exercises.explain: Content — layout and objects — is preserved while style is swapped.

exercises.prompt: Which best describes 'content' in style transfer?

options.text: The objects and spatial layout captured by deep CNN features

exercises.explain: Correct! Content is the structural layout captured by deep features.

exercises.prompt: A photographer wants to preserve exact object shapes but swap in a watercolor texture. Which part of the CNN features should dominate?

options.text: Content features should dominate to preserve shapes; style features add the watercolor texture
Only style features matter; content is irrelevant
Neither matters; only image resolution controls the outcome
The optimizer ignores both and copies pixels directly

exercises.explain: Exactly! Content preserves structure; style adds texture. Next: how the CNN actually separates them mathematically.
